title: Ops: Skills Monitoring & Commentary

use_cases.name: Run Skills Monitor
use_cases.description: Entry point: reads all skill directories, iterates and checks each, optionally auto-heals, returns issue report

use_cases.name: Check Individual Skill Health
use_cases.description: Validates a single skill directory: checks package.json validity, node_modules presence/emptiness, and SKILL.md existence

use_cases.name: Auto-Heal Skill Issues
use_cases.description: Attempts to fix detected issues: runs 'npm install --production' for missing/empty node_modules; creates SKILL.md stub for missing docs

use_cases.name: Load Skill Ignore List
use_cases.description: Reads .skill_monitor_ignore file from workspace root at module load time; hard-coded ignores: common, input-validator, proactive-agent, security-audit

use_cases.name: Generate Cycle Commentary
use_cases.description: Returns a persona-flavoured comment string for a cycle outcome (success or failure)

use_cases.name: Select Commentary Persona
use_cases.description: Chooses comment pool from three built-in personas: standard, greentea, maddog. Defaults to 'standard' if unknown persona supplied

use_cases.name: Report Unresolved Skill Issues
use_cases.description: CLI usage: prints JSON report of remaining issues to stdout, exits with code 1 if any issues remain, 0 otherwise

include_extend_relationships: UC1 includes UC3 (when autoHeal=true, extends UC2 result)
UC1 includes UC4 (at startup to filter skills)
UC5 includes UC6 (persona selection is part of getComment)
important_notes: autoHeal defaults to true (options.autoHeal !== false). Syntax checking via 'node -c' was intentionally removed for performance. node_modules emptiness check is used as a lightweight proxy for install validity. Commentary module is stateless — random selection from persona pool each call.
